title: Quararibea dowdingii
taxonomy: Quararibea dowdingii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the chafers or polypetalous family. It belongs to the genus Quararibea, which is a genus of tropical trees and shrubs. The plant is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. Quararibea dowdingii is a small tree with a broad, rounded crown and a straight, slender trunk. It is a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants.
uses: Quararibea dowdingii is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Quararibea dowdingii has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
